what_it_does: Defines a static HTML screen for composing a new piece, showing a progress ladder, frame archetype choices, three tuning dials, slot completion rows, a derived-values count strip, a stage action, and a generated icon rail.
observations: Loads Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts.
Uses inline CSS for the compose-specific ladder, archetype cards, dials, and slot rows.
Shows the flow state as root and role done, frame current, slots and voice pending.
Marks the stack archetype as selected among stack, duo, trio, and field.
Displays warmth, density, and register tracks with fixed knob positions and text values.
Lists six slots with four checked and two waiting.
Generates the bottom rail icons with DNA.org.icon from /surface/runtime/organisms.js.
standouts: The screen combines a wizard ladder with compositional archetype selection and parameter dials.
Slot rows read like a structured ledger of piece requirements and completion state.
The rail is rendered procedurally from a short icon-name list rather than hardcoded SVG markup.
conventions: Uses DNA design tokens through CSS custom properties for spacing, type, radius, and color.
Uses compact semantic-ish class names such as ladder, archeS, arch, dials, drow, and slotrow.
Uses div and span elements for visual controls instead of native form controls.
Uses inline style attributes for individual dial knob positions.
concerns: Interactive-looking controls are static div/span elements with no keyboard or form semantics in this file.
The script assumes a global DNA.org.icon API is present after loading /surface/runtime/organisms.js.
The page depends on absolute /surface paths and external font loading to render as intended.
notes: A compose-flow screen mockup centered on selecting a frame, tuning presentation attributes, and tracking required content slots.
questions: Is this intended to be a static visual state or an interactive compose step?
Should the archetype choices, dials, slot checks, and action be operable?
What does the affinity label represent in relation to the selected frame?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it presents a v1 compose-a-piece screen with the frame stage active and slot progress visible.
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to express composition as a guided, system-derived process where frame choice, tonal parameters, and required slots are visible in one phone-screen state.
novelty: The language treats UI construction as growth from a root, with slots, invariants, organisms, and derived values surfaced directly in the interface copy.
contribution: Contributes a mobile compose-screen substrate that preserves a staged workflow, archetype selection, tonal dial display, slot-completion ledger, derived-state messaging, and generated navigation rail.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML mobile compose screen with a progress ladder, selected stack frame, tuning dials, slot completion ledger, derived-values count strip, action control, and DNA-generated icon rail.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing a guided piece-composition step where structural frame, tone settings, and content slots are assembled from system-derived constraints.
